_provenance: Real evidence_ids + titles drawn from tests/fixtures/drb72/evidence_pool.json (DRB-EN #72, AI impact on labor markets). Baskets and section plans are the test SCENARIO built from that real data (LAW II real-data-only; the scenario grouping is the fixture, tests/fixtures exemption LAW VI). Exercises every map branch: multi-home, corroborating facet, tie-break -> lowest index, orphan -> residual (x2), sub-query-lineage signal, refuter-shape pass-through.
sub_queries: how does automation displace routine job tasks
does AI raise worker productivity and complement labor
which occupations are exposed to generative AI
empirical AI effects on employment wages and vacancies
policy responses and distributional effects of AI on labor

section_plans.title: Displacement and Task Automation
section_plans.focus: How automation and robots displace routine job tasks and lower the labor share.

section_plans.title: Productivity and Complementarity
section_plans.focus: Evidence that AI raises productivity and complements rather than replaces workers.

section_plans.title: Generative AI Exposure
section_plans.focus: Which occupations and tasks are exposed to large language models.

section_plans.title: Empirical Labor Market Outcomes
section_plans.focus: Empirical studies of AI effects on employment, wages, and vacancies.

section_plans.title: Policy and Distributional Effects
section_plans.focus: Policy responses and distributional consequences of AI on the labor market.

baskets.claim_text: Automation and new technology displace routine tasks and lower the labor share.
baskets.subject: automation

baskets.claim_text: Robot adoption reduces employment and wages in exposed local labor markets.
baskets.subject: robots

baskets.claim_text: Susceptibility estimates vary widely across studies.
baskets.subject: estimates

baskets.claim_text: Generative AI exposes a large share of occupations to task automation.
baskets.subject: generative AI

baskets.claim_text: Technological change historically created new jobs even as it destroyed old ones.
baskets.subject: technological change

baskets.claim_text: Firm level artificial intelligence adoption is associated with higher productivity.
baskets.subject: firms

baskets.claim_text: AI exposure appears in online job vacancy postings.
baskets.subject: vacancies

baskets.claim_text: The labor market effects of AI remain uncertain and contested.
baskets.subject: AI effects

baskets.claim_text: AI will disrupt the labor market but policy can shape the distributional outcome.
baskets.subject: policy

baskets.claim_text: An institutional review synthesizes distributional evidence.
baskets.subject: review

baskets.claim_text: The fourth industrial revolution framing shapes the automation debate.
baskets.subject: framing

ev_027.retrieval_subquery: policy responses and distributional effects of AI on labor
